<insights>

- Isolated files — no import edges between 3 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- Zero cohesion and zero coupling are expected for a zone composed entirely of binary images and an HTML file with no JS import graph.
- The light/dark mode logo pair indicates theme-aware design is implemented at the asset level.
- No code logic resides here — this zone is a pure asset bundle boundary.
- Cohesion and coupling metrics are not meaningful for zones composed entirely of static binary and HTML assets — the zone is correctly isolated.
- The presence of both lightmode and darkmode logo variants confirms intentional theme support; ensure CSS or JS theme-switching logic in web-dashboard correctly references both assets.
- index.html in this zone likely serves as the bundler entry for the viewer app — if it references viewer/polling or other viewer modules, those dependencies are resolved by the bundler and will not appear in the import graph.
- viewer-static-assets is structurally symmetric with landing-page: both are 3-file, zero-coupling terminal zones composed of non-JS assets — this confirms the community detector correctly routes binary and HTML assets to isolated zones rather than merging them with their logical owners.
- viewer-static-assets and landing-page are structurally identical terminal zones — if either grows to include TypeScript logic, the community detector will automatically migrate those files into the appropriate code zone on the next analysis run.
